items.title: Partial derivatives
items.description: Each component of ∇f is a partial derivative; assembling them into a vector adds direction and steepest-ascent meaning.

items.title: Chain rule
items.description: Backpropagation applies the chain rule to accumulate partial derivatives into gradient vectors for nested computations.

items.title: Gradient descent
items.description: Optimization algorithms use −∇L(θ) as the local search direction when minimizing loss over parameters θ.

items.title: Loss surfaces
items.description: ∇L at a point on a loss surface gives the local slope vector—how loss changes when parameters move infinitesimally.
